items.description: Modern black ink inscription 'Codex Regius 2365.' in a neat formal hand at the top of the page.
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval shelfmark or photographic identification label added during cataloguing or digitization; not original to the 13th-century manuscript.

items.description: Faint irregular grayish smudge, roughly 4-5 cm wide, in the lower left area with soft, diffuse edges.
items.scholarly_note: Water stain, foxing, or handling abrasion typical of long-term storage and use of parchment; no deliberate marking.

items.description: Vertical series of small punctures and dark stains along the right edge/gutter, including at least five distinct holes or spots with black accretions, plus a larger irregular blot near the lower right corner.
items.scholarly_note: Sewing or stab holes from the original or later binding of the codex. Dark marks result from accumulated dirt, thread residue, or offset from adjacent material over centuries.

items.description: Small isolated circular black ink dot, approximately 1-2 mm, located in the right half of the page about one-third of the way down and inset from the edge.
items.scholarly_note: Possible pen trial (nib or ink test) or accidental ink drop. No accompanying strokes, letters, or flourishes visible to confirm deliberate practice.

scratches: Faint irregular surface abrasion and scuffing co-located with the lower left stain.
damage_notes: Multiple sewing/stab holes with soiling concentrated along the right binding margin.
Minor edge wear, small material losses, and dark staining at the lower right corner of the leaf.
The leaf surface is otherwise exceptionally clean and bright with no visible rulings, text, or medieval ink.
structuredOutput.summary: Page 117 of Codex Regius presents as a blank leaf bearing no original medieval text, marginalia, doodles, figurative marks, decorations, or scribal corrections. The only prominent added mark is a modern shelfmark inscription at the top. Visible non-textual features are limited to a faint lower-left stain and typical binding damage consisting of punctures and dark accretions along the right gutter and edge, plus one small isolated ink spot. The page is remarkably pristine for a c. 1270 Icelandic parchment leaf.
